title: potentilla longibracteata
intro: Potentilla longibracteata, also known as the long-bracted cinquefoil, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its berries can be used to make jams and other edible products.
